Blog posts support Docusaurus Markdown features, such as MDX. تلميح Use the power of React to create interactive blog posts. For example, use JSX to create an interactive button: <button onClick={() => alert('button clicked!')}>Click me!</button> Click me!

Docusaurus blogging features are powered by the blog plugin. Here are a few tips you might find useful. Simply add Markdown files (or folders) to the blog directory. Regular blog authors can be added to authors.yml. The blog post date can be extracted from filenames, such as: 2019-05-30-welcome.md 2019-05-30-welcome/index.md A blog post folder can be convenient to co-locate blog post images: The blog supports tags as well! And if you don't want a blog: just delete this directory, and use blog: false in your Docusaurus config.

This tab covers external scholarships — funding offered by NGOs, private foundations, and government programs that students in Lebanon can apply to in addition to any aid offered by their university. (Scholarships offered by a specific university live on that university's page in the Universities tab.)

Every scholarship page follows the same six sections so you can compare opportunities quickly: Overview — provider, type, and a one-paragraph description. Grade & background requirements — academic level, GPA, nationality / residency, financial need, and any demographic or major restrictions. Application window — when applications open, when they close, and the decision timeline. Supported universities — which universities the scholarship can be used at. Benefits — what is covered (tuition %, housing, stipend, books, travel, mentorship). Contacts of recipients — past recipients who agreed to answer questions, plus the official program contact. This is the most useful section: real people who have been through the process and can give you honest advice.
documents.s: How each scholarship page is organized

Welcome. This tab is your starting point for choosing and applying to a university in Lebanon. Each university listed here has its own self-contained page, so once you pick a school you can read everything you need without hopping between articles.

Every university page follows the same five sections, in the same order, so you can compare schools side by side: Overview — general information, founding year, campuses, language of instruction, notable alumni, and the latest rankings. Majors — most popular and most common majors offered, grouped by faculty, with references to the official program pages. Application — application types, application windows and deadlines, application fees, and the requirements to join (SAT range, high-school grades, English proficiency, program-specific extras). Tuition & Scholarships — tuition cost overview plus all scholarships offered by the university itself (merit, financial aid, need-based grants, work-study, athletic and talent awards). Contacts — phone numbers and emails for the admissions office, financial aid office, and individual departments. Looking for external scholarships from NGOs, foundations, or government programs? Those live in the Scholarships tab.
documents.s: How each university page is organized
